rulings.Case Title: TIAN SHAN TRADING (USA), INC., A CALIFORNIA VS. NANCY HUANG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside The Entries Of Default
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 17, 2010, line 4. DEFENDANTS TIN YUN, CELLULAR GOPHER, L.L.C.'S Motion To Set Aside The Entries Of Default is granted. This motion is timely under C.C.P. section 473.5. No default judgment has been entered and plaintiff fails to state when the service of notice of entry of judgment was effectuated. Yum, who operates the L.L.C., states he never had notice of the action untill plaintiff served its motion to vacate dismissal.

rulings.Case Title: BRET LUEDER VS. BAY AREA TOLL AUTHORITY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 17, 2010, line 9. DEFENDANTS CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES, DMV DIRECTOR GEORGE VALVERDE'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. No opposition filed. Plaintiff must plead a viable federal due process claim. Plaintiff must allege compliance with the Tort Claims Act (see Government Code section 800 et seq) and must have filed a claim before filing his lawsuit (see G.C. section 911.2 and 945.4. Plaintiff must allege a statutory basis of liability against defendants. (G.C. section 815, 815.2) There is no common-law liability against the state. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: FAYE BEYELER VS. SARAH BEYELER et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 17, 2010, line 10. DEFENDANTS SARAH BEYELER, JONATHAN BEYELER, ROCHELLE BEYELER'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to 2nd cause of action for concealment. Plaintiff must allege a duty to disclose a fact to plaintiff or a fiduciary relationship, and defendants' intent to defraud. Demurrer overruled as to remaining causes of action. =(302/CWW)
